observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to immediately conduct physical count on these balances and report the same in the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report of the Physical Count of the Biological Assets (RPCBA)and copy furnished the Audit Team and Accounting Office. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to reclassify the Work/Zoo Animals account to Breeding Stocks and to reconcile the RPCI and RPCBA with the accounting records and prepare adjustments, if appropriate.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to immediately prepare journal vouchers to adjust the Advances to Officer and Employees account as follows: Debit Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Credit Advances to Officers and Employees by the same amount, ₱4,423,021.10, to record the cash advance for Confidential Fund which was erroneously recorded as Advances to Officers and Employees; Debit Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Credit Advances to Officers and Employees by the same amount, ₱250,000.00, to record the cash advance for Special Purpose which was erroneously recorded as Advances to Officers and Employees; and Debit Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Credit Advances to Officers and Employees by the same amount, ₱39,000.00, to record the cash advance for Special Purpose which was erroneously recorded as cash advance for travel.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasure to open two (2) separate depository accounts for the Municipal DRRMF share from regular sources and the Special Trust Fund through Sangguniang Bayan resolution. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers for every year of transfer of unutilized balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to Trust Fund books and disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the DRRMF in adherence to Sections 5.1.10, 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management strengthen the installation of an effective inventory management system to make sound analysis on the receipt, distribution and/ or utilization of in-kind donations by adhering COA Circular No. 2014-002 as amended by COA Circular No. 2020-009. Require the concerned offices for the receipt and distribution to submit immediately a Summary/List of Donations received, Distributed and Balances and the Accounting Office and the Property Unit to record timely the in-kind donations in the books or in the registries.
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the remittance of the amount to the National Treasury.

We recommended that Management require the members of the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all PPEs in three months or less and require them to submit an updated RPCPPE after completion of the physical count. Direct the General Services Office and Accounting Office to update and reconcile the Property Cards and PPELC.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to inform in writing those payees with stale checks totaling ₱206,712.71                     to return the stale checks to the Municipal Treasurer or to submit a sworn statement if the checks were lost in adherence with Sections 59 and 58 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Otherwise, these claims are forfeited in favor of the Municipality. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accounting Office a) to review the unrecorded reconciling items and the variance of ₱132,792.19 and to effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and b) to prepare and submit the BRS on four (4) depository bank accounts in the Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to submit the RCDs, with copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips, for the months of October to December 2021 and require the MAO to submit the same within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month. Likewise, ensure that the Municipal Treasurer and all accountable officers                 who received and issued with accountable forms for collections made for the Municipality prepare monthly RAAF and the Municipal Treasurer submits the Consolidated RAAF with the individual monthly RAAFs not later than the                   fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to continue sending demand letters to all concerned individuals for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances. Otherwise, instruct the  Municipal Accountant (a) to deduct from the salaries of the concerned personnel who are still connected with the Municipality who still fail                            to liquidate, or perform all means for the collection of the outstanding amount; and (b) to appropriately classify the special purpose and confidential fund to Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account.

We recommended that Management create a position for General Services Officer who will be in-charge of the establishment and management of an effective inventory system. Likewise, the Management comply with Sections 111 and 112 of PD 1445, and Sections 114, 119, 121 and 124 of LGU NGAS Manual Volume 1 to properly record, control, and monitor the purchase, receipt, acceptance and issuance of inventory items.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of the copy of certificate of acceptance and turnover report of completed national government funded projects. Further, expedite the remittance of the ₱4,000,000.00 PDAF-related liability to the National Treasury. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to review and make the necessary adjustments to correct the negative balances and improper classification of the accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to open two (2) separate depository accounts for the Municipal DRRMF share from regular sources and the Special Trust Fund through Sangguniang Bayan resolution. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain subsidiary ledgers for every year of transfer of unutilized balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to Trust Fund books and to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the DRRMF in adherence to Sections 5.1.10, 5.1.12 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant                         to disclose all the available information on the Other Payables account in the Notes to Financial Statements for full transparency.

We recommended that Management review the viability and necessity of the unimplemented development projects as of December 31, 2021 and update the Annual Investment Program of CY 2022, if necessary; and ensure that the projects are fully implemented within the time frame.

We recommended that Management instruct the General Services Office-Designate to notify the Audit Team of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries of supplies and goods aside from the date of delivery indicated in the PO. Moreover, ensure that all the required information in the POs are completely filled-up.

We recommended that Management strengthen the installation of an effective inventory management system to make sound analysis on the receipt, distribution and/ or utilization of in-kind donations by adhering COA Circular No. 2014-002 as amended by COA Circular No. 2020-009. Require the concerned offices for the receipt and distribution to submit immediately a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and the Accounting Office and the Property Unit to record timely the in-kind donations in the books or in the registries.

We recommended that Management direct the General Services Office-Designate to consolidate the purchase request needed at or about the same time to avoid splitting of contracts/ purchase orders in compliance with Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 and                                the Bids and Awards Committee to strengthen the review of purchase requests before the conduct of procurement.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to enforce the intense collection of delinquent accounts on real property and special education taxes for calendar years 2002 to 2021 totaling ₱9,203,651.12 by adhering Article 345 of the IRR of RA 7160 and to furnish, at the beginning of the year, the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) of the Certified List showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in compliance of Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUS, Volume I. Likewise, the MAO record accordingly the receivables using the modified accrual accounting as provided in Section 19 (b) of the same Manual.

We recommended that Management temporarily relieve the Inventory Committee from their regular duties to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less, and to submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking; and ensure full implementation of "One-time cleansing of PPE accounts" as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to record procurement of supplies and relief goods in the appropriate Inventory account and recognized the Expense account when the goods are distributed. Require the Accounting Office, General Service Office and LDRRM Office to prepare the required records and reports as provided for in COA Circular No. 2014-002 and GAM for LGUs, Volume I to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to immediately submit the Monthly Pre-Closing Trial Balance and Trial Balance for CY 2020 and the Budget Office on the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for the period January to September 2020. Likewise, adhere Section 361 and 362 of GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit immediately the mentioned lacking necessary documents and ensure strict compliance with the prescribed procurement procedures in RA 9184 and COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that Management direct all concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances by sending them demand letters. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct from the salaries of the concerned officials/personnel with long outstanding cash advance if they still fail to liquidate and coordinate with the family of the deceased or perform all means for the collection of the outstanding amount.

We recommended that Management require the members of the BAC to equip and update themselves of the existing GPPB guidelines which are exempted from the provisions of RA No. 9184 in response to COVID-19 outbreak. Submit immediately the Omnibus Sworn Statement and any of those mentioned in Item 5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.

We recommended that Management ensure to create special account when the guidelines for every fund required to do so. Require the Local Finance Committee to submit immediately the lacking monthly Fund Utilization Report and Status of Implementation of PPAs and the succeeding reports be timely available to the public in adherence to the prescribed guidelines outlined in the LBC No. 125; and if opening of an official website is not possible, provide alternative actions to post online the reports for viewing purposes.

We recommended that Management direct the Local Development Council and Municipal Engineer to properly plan and review the project proposals submitted and to ensure prioritization of funding on development projects that are readily doable and without unresolved issue/s to prevent delay implementation; and establish internal control measures by conducting timely coordination, regular monitoring and evaluation of every development project.

We recommended that MDRMM Office through MDRMM Council maximize the utilization of the Special Trust Fund and Continuing Appropriation by considering the priorities, needs and desired outcomes of the Municipality and its constituents through incorporation of the programs and projects in the annual and/or investment plan; and intensify the monitoring and evaluation of its implementation to strengthen the Municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that Management direct the General Service Office to consolidate the purchase request needed at or about the same time to avoid splitting of contracts/ purchase orders in compliance with Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 76-41 and the Bids and Awards Committee to strengthen the review of purchase request before conduct of procurement.

We recommended that Management assist the Department of Education (DepEd) in the efficient and effective implementation of the National Feeding Program’s components and coverage of RA 11037 using a portion of the SEF in recognition of the relationship between food and nutrition and the capacity of the students to develop and learn.
We recommend that Management expedite the implementation of the six (6) projects funded out of the Share from National Wealth to achieve socio-economic benefits at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit immediately the reviewed Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the corresponding Accomplishment Report (AR) for calendar year 2020 and the GAD Focal submit to the Audit Team the succeeding GPB within five (5) working days from the receipt of the reviewed GPB and the AR within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate depository account for GAD Fund through Sangguniang Bayan resolution.

We recommended that Management strictly implement their policy with regard to delinquencies. Also, intensify collection efforts by sending collection/demand letters to delinquent concessionaires. Otherwise, resort to the issuance of notice of disconnection in order to avoid further increase of their delinquencies.

We recommended that the Management should require the Municipal Accountant to verify and inform the banks on the reconciling items that need to be corrected by them and effect the necessary adjustments by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher. Henceforth, immediate verification on unusual credits and debits appearing in the bank statements shall be made and the corresponding adjustment in the book of accounts shall also be taken.

We recommended that Management should (i) trace these dormant accounts to available accounting records or make representation with possible source agencies in order to identify such and return the unutilized funds; and (ii) monitor the recording and utilization of funds to avoid recurrence of the same deficiency.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the concerned Accounting and Budget personnel to (i) stop immediately the remittance of Pag-IBIG Fund government share contribution in excess of the prescribed rate; (ii) adhere strictly to the laws/regulations on the Pag-IBIG Fund Contributions (government share) as provided in RA 9679 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, respectively; and (b) require the employees/officials concerned to refund the excess amount of the government share contribution.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal ENRO in strict compliance to the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsite as provided in RA 9003 to avoid applicable sanctions towards erring officials.

We recommended that Management (a) advise the concerned officials to conduct the estimation of revenue based on the previous years’ actual revenue realized; (b) conduct LSB planning and budgeting compliant to DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017; and (c) inform LSB on the available accumulated fund for additional projects/activities thru issuance of supplemental appropriation ordinance.

We recommended that Management (a) undertake immediate remedial measure to make the structure operational, otherwise this may cause audit disallowance for the total cost of the project; (b) direct the contractor’s workers to vacate the slaughter house and establish their own campsite for workers.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer and other concerned officials to: (i) prioritize the conduct in-depth analysis of the accumulated cash balances to identify trust liabilities and funds free from obligations; (ii) study and discuss among themselves opportunity to earn additional income thru prudent investment of idle funds; and (iii) if feasible, prepare a proposal for investment; (b) coordinate to the officials of authorized depository banks for further understanding of investment programs offered that helps in effective decision making; and (c) submit the proposal to the Sangguniang Bayan for its review and approval.

We recommended that Management (a) re-evaluate the system, document all problem encountered and communicate in writing to the ECPAC provider for the immediate enhancement of the system; (b) determine if there is a need to continue the subscription of the Accounting System considering the deficiencies noted and the prompt response of the ECPAC Provider to address the same. If so, execute a contract that fully defined the desired services and outputs of the said system, such as but not limited to: (i) consistency of data in the ledgers, statements and other templates/outputs; (ii) provision of user’s manual, system’s flow diagram and other related ECPAC Accounting System tools; (iii) availability of facilities which are already featured in the system as raised in item c above or in Annex M and the facility for data extraction; (iv) frequency of visitation for monitoring the efficiency and continuity of the system’s operations; and (v) reasonable contract period considering that the government will be providing a uniform accounting system in the near future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) advised the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow Sections 70, 72, and 73 of the New Government Accounting System for the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports. This will ensure timeliness of reports by the LGU as well as the auditor. We further recommended to the LCE to initiate the filing of appropriate charges, when necessary to officials who refused or for unjustified failure to effect the compliance of the said regulations.

We further recommended to the LCE to initiate the filing of appropriate charges, when necessary to officials who refused or for unjustified failure to effect the compliance of the said regulations.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Accountant to send demand letters to all accountable officers for the settlement of their outstanding cash advances; otherwise ensure to withhold the concerned employees’ salaries in accordance with paragraph 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and (b) exert efforts to retrieve required documents and continue with the preparation of request for write-off of those outstanding balances from past administrations.

We recommended that Management (a) draw a JEV by debiting Furniture and Fixtures account, and credit to Construction in Progress account and for the corresponding depreciation expense, and submit to the Office of the Auditor as soon as possible; and (b) perform thorough review of the accounting entries upon posting JEVs to avoid recurring the same circumstance.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to submit the mentioned lacking necessary documents and ensure to establish a sound internal control measures by observing the processes and review of documentations required in all procurement activities.

We recommended that the Management give equal priority in each thematic area of disaster management by allocating sufficient funds in each to ensure that the local needs for disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery are met.

We recommended that Management ensure to effectively install the necessary specific and monitoring controls relative to the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption which should be reasonably utilized and completely documented.
We also recommended that the following be considered in the controls to be installed:
The approved trip ticket be serially numbered and prepared in two copies, original-attach to the claims for fuel, duplicate-file. Completely fill up the DTT indicating the specific names of authorized passengers and their signatures, specific purpose of the trip, detailed daily itinerary of travel indicating the time and place of departure and arrival, speedometer/odometer reading, total kilometers travelled and gasoline consumed. If the speedometer is out of order, indicate the daily specific point of origin and destination. The concerned GSO personnel should check the accuracy of the data appearing in the DTT whenever necessary particularly the ending balances compared to the beginning balance in tank of the succeeding DTT; and
The Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared by the driver concerned summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month. For each vehicle, one report shall be prepared monthly. The report shall be certified by the driver and approved by Chief, Municipal GSO. This should be accomplished in three (3) copies, original-Auditor, Duplicate-Administrative Officer, Triplicate-File.

We recommended that Management strictly implement their policy with regard to delinquencies. Also, intensify collection efforts by sending collection/demand letters to delinquent concessionaires. Otherwise, resort to the issuance of notice of disconnection in order to avoid further increase of their delinquencies.

We recommended that Management (i) prepare comprehensive guidelines and specific policies and procedures concerning the grants of financial assistance; and (ii) faithfully adhere to the intent of Section 2 of PD 1445 in the implementation of any program of the LGU.

We recommended that Management (a) require the immediate submission of the Approved Application for Leave and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to support the CY 2019 paid monetization claims; (b) ensure to implement and/or initiate control measures to facilitate the employees’ timely submission of DTRs and its attachments and thereon, judiciously effect the recording of transactions on the employees leave cards; and (c) submit authenticated copies of updated leave cards of all employees to the Office of the Auditor as soon as possible.

We recommend that Management ensure to direct responsible employees’ compliance on the required technical documents such as approved program of work/plan prior the project’s appropriation.
Likewise, assure to establish internal control measures by conducting timely coordination, regular monitoring and evaluation of every development project to ensure that resources are managed in the most effective and efficient manner to meet the needs of its constituents.

We further recommended to the LCE to initiate the filing of appropriate charges to officials who refused or for unjustified failure to effect the compliance of the said regulations.

We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to use the Accrual Basis of Accounting in recognizing the income/revenue based on the projected revenue for the year from waterworks system operation to be prepared by MEEDO.

We recommended that the municipal government should prepare an APP thru the GSO and the BAC for recommendation of appropriate procurement modality and remind the different departments/ offices of their duty to submit the required Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for their programs, activities and projects as basis for budgeting and consolidation to APP to have a centralized and organized procurement of goods for various departments/offices.

We recommended that the Management should:
Direct the MENRO to expedite the preparation of the required additional requirements to complete the plan so that immediate submission to NSWMC could be done;
Comply strictly with all provisions of the RA No. 9003, in the formulation and implementation of the plans, in order to achieve the intended purpose of the law; and
Monitor religiously the implementation of programs and activities embodied in their SWMP to ensure protection of public health and the environment and to comply with the provisions under the RA 9003 otherwise known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
We recommended that Management shall direct the Local School Board to ensure appropriations of SEF funds for the implementation of ECCD Program in accordance with Section 7(b) of Republic Act. 10410.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant to obtain the details of the balance of the due to BIR account and strictly observe the BIR due dates on the remittances of taxes withheld to avoid payment of interests, penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the Management require program of works, site investigation, and regular monitoring and evaluation for every development projects to ensure that resources are managed in the most effective and efficient manner to meet the needs of its constituents.

We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management has to take appropriate steps in requiring strict settlement of accounts of concerned officials and employees.  The ninety (90) days prescribed period must be observed to comply with the requirements of the transactions suspended in audit.  Settlement of disallowances must immediately be effected by withholding the salaries or other claims of persons liable in satisfaction of the amount disallowed.

The Accountant should (i) adhere to the regulations on the preparation and submission of the financial statements as provided in Section 474(b) of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2010-001; and (ii) submit the FSs as soon as these are available to the COA for timely review of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Accountant to (i) adhere to the regulations on the preparation and submission of the financial statements as provided in Section 474(b) of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2010-001; and (ii) submit the FSs as soon as these are available to the COA for timely review of the accounts.
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjustments on the reconciling items by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher using the appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Management should:
create an Inventory Team to conduct the physical count of PPE accounts; prepare the prescribed physical count report as soon as the physical count is completed; and furnish copy of the report to the Accountant and COA;
advise all heads of offices to coordinate the conduct of physical count by preparing all property records assigned to their respective office and its physical existence in order to fast track the undertaking; and
direct all concerned officials to maintain the appropriate ledger and property cards and adhere the regulations of property management as mentioned above to avoid possible disciplinary action against those erring officials for neglect of duty.

We therefore reiterate our previous recommendation and Management agreed to advise the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert extra effort to retrieve and establish the details of information of the dormant accounts; (b) demand settlement from the accountable officers/concerned personnel; (c) prepare a written request for write-off of receivable accounts for responsible persons that could not be located after exhausting extra effort.

We therefore reiterate our previous recommendations and  Management agreed to:
direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to (i) perform a physical count of inventories in order to establish the actual inventory on-hand for all offices under the Municipal Government of Tagana-an; (ii) consolidate all inventory on-hand; and (iii) coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the reconciliation of inventory accounts to arrive at the accurate and actual amounts;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) comply PPSAS 12, that is, record the purchases of supplies and materials as inventory and recognize expense when the inventories are consumed or distributed; (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Inventory Committee in the reconciliation of inventory accounts; and (iii) prepare JEV to adjust the reconciling items; and
designate competent personnel to handle the general services particularly to (i) perform the custodianship of supplies and materials; and (ii) prepare RSMI and submit to the Accountant for the recognition of expense account in the consumption or distribution of inventories.

We recommended that Management should (i) trace these dormant accounts to available accounting records or make representation with possible source agencies in order to identify such and return the unutilized funds; and (ii) monitor the recording and utilization of funds to avoid recurrence of the same deficiency.

We reiterate to recommend and Management agreed to:
direct all the concerned officials/employees to (i) immediately settle the outstanding cash advances; and (ii) all advances must be liquidated at year-end and thereafter;
instruct the Accountant to (i) deduct from the salaries of the concerned officials/personnel the long outstanding advances if they fail to settle at due time; (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless otherwise the previous advance is liquidated; and (iii) coordinate with national agencies top management to direct those officials/employees previously assigned in the municipality to immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and
file a request for authority to write-off the receivables to COA Regional Director with complete supporting documents, if after exhausting all means failed to locate the inactive personnel and family of deceased accountable officers; (c) adhere to the regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare JEV for the reclassification of Work/Zoo Animals to its appropriate account; and
present the Breeding Stocks at its fair value less cost to sell at reporting date for a PPSAS-compliant financial statements, with the assistance of Municipal Agriculturist.
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the remittance of the amount to the National Treasury.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of the 20 percent Development Fund and; (ii) to strictly adhere to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 in the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund, otherwise this may result to audit disallowance.

We recommended that Management should:
comply with the laws, rules and regulations in granting honoraria to government personnel;
stop the practice of granting honoraria to the officials of the PNP and BFP, and Punong Barangays, otherwise this may result to an audit disallowance; and
require concerned officials to refund the honorarium received.

We recommended for the Management to:
direct the concerned Accounting and Budget personnel to (i) stop immediately the remittance of Pag-IBIG Fund government share contribution in excess of the prescribed rate; (ii) adhere strictly to the laws/regulations on the Pag-IBIG Fund Contributions (government share) as provided in RA 9679 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, respectively; and
require the employees/officials concerned to refund the excess amount of the government share contribution.

We recommended that the CFC shall (i) intensify review of the Barangays’ annual budget to consider compliance to budgetary and other requirements as prescribed in RAs 7160, 9344, 10121, PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular 2012-01 and DBM/DSWD Joint Circular No. 2003-01; and (ii) advise all barangays the prescribed allocation of funds of the said programs.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Accountant should (i) adhere to the regulations for the submission of Report of Disbursement (ROD)/Liquidation to the Source Agencies; (ii) establish monitoring control of receipts, utilization and liquidation of the accounts; and (iii) any savings will be returned to the source agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and MDRR Officer to strictly observe the preparation and submission of monthly report of LDRRMF utilization as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal ENRO in strict compliance to the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsite as provided in RA 9003 to avoid applicable sanctions towards erring officials.

We recommended that Management:
advise the concerned officials to conduct the estimation of revenue based on the previous years’ actual revenue realized;
conduct LSB planning and budgeting compliant to DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017; and
inform LSB on the available accumulated fund for additional projects/activities thru issuance of supplemental appropriation ordinance.

We therefore reiterated to recommend Management to:
intensify planning of proposed projects to (i) consider the most priority that should be accomplished for the timely use of the projects without sacrificing the quality of work; (ii) conduct effective survey of project locations in order to prepare the appropriate plans and specifications; (iii) consider the available materials in the market; and (iii) ensure the locations are owned by the government;
consider hiring additional technical personnel in Engineering Office to fast tract the unattended workloads; and
enhance monitoring control in the implementation projects including the procurement process to immediately address problems encountered that could affect its timely completion.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer and other concerned officials to: (i) prioritize the conduct in-depth analysis of the accumulated cash balances to identify trust liabilities and funds free from obligations; (ii) study and discuss among themselves opportunity to earn additional income thru prudent investment of idle funds; and (iii) if feasible, prepare a proposal for investment;
coordinate to the officials of authorized depository banks for further understanding of investment programs offered that helps in effective decision making; and
submit the proposal to the Sangguniang Bayan for its review and approval.

We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management has to take appropriate steps in requiring strict settlement of accounts of concerned officials and employees.  The ninety (90) days prescribed period must be observed to comply with the requirements of the transactions suspended in audit.  Settlement of disallowances must immediately be effected by withholding the salaries or other claims of persons liable in satisfaction of the amount disallowed.

2.10 	We reiterate to recommend that Management direct the Treasurer and the Accountant to:
(a) 	strictly adhere to the regulations on Real Property Tax as provided in Section 246 of RA 7160; and
(b) advise the Treasurer and the Accountant to (i) coordinate in the setting up of the Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Income by providing sufficient data as basis in the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to record the accrual of income; and (ii) thereafter, continue the process in every first day of the year.

3.10	We recommended that Management:
(a)	direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to(i) perform a physical count of inventories in order to establish the actual inventory on-hand for all offices under the Municipal Government of Tagana-an; (ii)consolidate all inventory on-hand; and (iii)coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the reconciliation of inventory accounts to arrive at the accurate and actual amounts;
(b) 	instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) comply PPSAS 12, that is, record the purchases of supplies and materials as inventory and recognize expense when the inventories are consumed or distributed; (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Inventory Committee in the reconciliation of inventory accounts; and (iii) prepare JEV to adjust the reconciling items; and
(c)	instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) perform the custodianship of supplies and materials, that is, all purchases should be delivered in Treasury Office as his additional duty provided in local government code; and (ii) prepare RSMI to support the recognition of expense account in the consumption or distribution of inventories.

5.9 	We recommended that Management
(a) submit immediately justification for the (i) advance payment made to contractor; (ii) changes of the specifications of the goods procured; and (ii) inclusion of motor engine in the service contract;
(b) 	attach sufficient appropriate supporting documents of the justifications; and
(c) 	adhere to the regulations stipulated under Section 88(1) of PD 1445 and Section 53 of RA 9184.

6.10 	We recommended that Management
(a) 	advise the Municipal Agriculturist to (i) complete the Physical Inventory count of the living animals dispersed; and (ii) submit an updated monitoring report to the Municipal Accountant for appropriate recognition in accordance to PPSAS 27; and
(b) 	direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV to reclassify the Work/Zoo Animals account to its appropriate account.

7.12 	We recommended that Management
(a) 	intensify planning of proposed projects to (i) consider the most priority that should be accomplished for the timely use of the projects without sacrificing the quality of work; (ii) conduct effective survey of project locations in order to prepare the appropriate plans and specifications; (iii) consider the available materials in the market; and (iv) ensure the locations are owned by the government;
(b) 	consider hiring additional technical personnel in Engineering Office to fast tract the unattended workloads; and
(c) 	enhance monitoring control in the implementation projects including the procurement process to immediately address problems encountered that could affect its timely completion.

8.8 	We reiterated to recommend that Management
(a) 	adhere to the regulations on real property tax as provided in Articles 346 and 362 of the IRR of  RA 7160;
(b) 	instruct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct more intense action for the collection of delinquent accounts by prompting the issuance of notices to defaulting taxpayers and warrant of levy before the public auction; and
(c) 	advise the Accountant to motivate the barangay officials to assist in collecting the delinquent accounts of property taxes located at their concerned barangays by prompting the release of share of property tax collected duly supported with template on the computation of shares.

10.10	We recommended that Management
(a) 	immediate remedial measure should be engaged to make this structure operational, otherwise this may cause audit disallowance amounting P1,397,625.00; and
(b) 	strengthen the monitoring control on infrastructure projects by conducting an effective regular inspection on site and timely submission of reports by a reliable personnel to immediately address the defects.

13.7	We recommended that Management
comply with the regulation on the budget allocation of one percent (1%) from the IRA for the LCPC programs/activities/projects; and
submit Executive Order issued by the Municipal Mayor for the establishment of the Council, approved Annual Work and Financial Plan and other pertinent documents as stipulated in Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

23. 	We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management has to take appropriate steps in requiring strict settlement of accounts of concerned municipal officials and employees.  The ninety (90) days prescribed period must be observed to comply with the requirements of the transactions suspended in audit.  Settlement of disallowances must immediately be effected by withholding the salaries or other claims of persons liable in satisfaction of the amount disallowed.
